data.title: Finland joins declaration on two-state solution between Israel, Palestinians
data.description: Finland is joining the French-Saudi New York declaration on a peaceful resolution of the Palestinian question and implementation of a two-state solution, the Nordic country said on Friday.

data.title: Gold Edges Higher After Record Run as Traders Await U.S. Jobs Data — Market Talk
data.description: Gold prices edge higher in early trade after pausing from a record-breaking rally as traders locked in profits ahead of the release of key U.S. nonfarm payrolls data. Futures are up 0.1% to $3,610.40 a troy ounce and on track for a 2.6% weekly gain, after hitting a new high of $3,640.10/oz earlier this week. The precious metal's surge was largely driven by expectations that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates at its next meeting, concerns about the U.S. central bank's independence following President Trump's move to fire Fed governor Lisa Cook, and persistent geopolitical risks. Now, "the biggest risk from a market perspective is an upside surprise to today's payrolls number," says Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB. "If rate cut bets get scaled back, then inflation fears could ease, which may hurt the world's most famous inflation hedge." (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: European Stocks Cautious, US Jobs Awaited
data.description:
European stocks were cautious on Friday, with the STOXX 50 and STOXX 600 both swinging around the flatline, ahead of the closely watched US jobs report, which could shape expectations for a potential Fed rate cut later this month.

In macro news, German factory orders fell 2.9% in July, the steepest drop since January and a reminder of persistent weakness in industrial demand.

On the corporate front, ASML gained over 2%, while Safran rose 1% after reports it is exploring the sale of much of its aircraft interiors division.

Orsted added nearly 1%, despite cutting its 2025 operating profit outlook, just ahead of a shareholder vote on a key capital increase.

Meanwhile, SAP (-0.5%), LVMH (-0.5%), Novo Nordisk (-0.2%), and Nestlé (-0.4%) were among the notable decliners.

data.title: DAX on Cautious Trading
data.description:
The DAX in Frankfurt traded cautiously on Friday, edging up 0.1% to around 23,790, in line with its peers, in line with its peers, as investors awaited the highly anticipated U.S. jobs report for August, due later in the day.

Signs of a further slowdown in the labor market appear increasingly likely, following weaker-than-expected private sector data released on Thursday—reinforcing expectations of a potential Fed rate cut within the next two weeks.

Meanwhile, traders also weighed the implications of Porsche AG’s upcoming exit from Germany’s DAX index on September 22, 2025.

The decision, prompted by a 30% year-to-date decline in the company’s stock and ongoing structural headwinds—including U.S. tariffs on European imports and sluggish demand in China—highlights deeper vulnerabilities within the German automotive sector.

data.title: Copper rises on China supply concerns; investors eye US jobs data
data.description:
Copper prices posted modest gains on Friday, supported by a rare decline in refined copper production in top producer China, while investors kept an eye on key U.S. jobs data.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.55% at $9,952.5 per metric ton, as of 0706 GMT, and on track to end the week 0.54% higher, if the current trend holds.

The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange gained 0.2% to 80,140 yuan ($11,203.85) a ton, ending the week 0.91% higher.

China's refined copper production is forecast for a rare fall this month amid newly introduced tax regulations to constrain the supply of scrap copper.

Despite sluggish operating rates and weaker-than-expected imports driven by shifts in scrap copper policy, analysts from Everbright Futures expect a strong rebound in refined copper consumption.

Meanwhile, BYD , , the world's largest electric vehicle manufacturer, slashed its sales target for the year by as much as 16% to 4.6 million vehicles from its estimate of 5.5 million vehicles in March.

Copper is widely used in electric vehicles and charging infrastructure.

On the other hand, the dollar index , which measures the U.S. currency against six major peers, dipped 0.1% to 98.14, though it was up 0.4% for the week.

A weaker dollar makes greenback-denominated assets like copper more affordable to holders of other currencies.

Focus will also be on the U.S. non-farm payrolls data, due at 1230 GMT, that could offer more clarity on the Fed's interest rate trajectory.

Among other London metals, aluminium rose 0.46%, lead climbed 0.18%, tin gained 0.23%, zinc increased 0.53%, and nickel was up 0.03%.

SHFE aluminium edged 0.29% higher, lead rose 0.21%, zinc increased 0.09%, while nickel dipped 0.16%, and tin lost 0.12%.

For the top stories in metals and other news, click

or

($1 = 7.1529 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Austria Wholesale Prices Rebound in August
data.description:
Wholesale prices in Austria rose by 0.2% year-on-year in August 2025, rebounding from a 0.1% drop in the previous month.

The growth was driven by significant increases in costs for coffee, tea, cocoa and spices (+24.7%), watches and jewelry (+18.9%), live animals (+18.5%) and non-alcoholic beverages (+9.6%).

Upward pressures also came from fruits and vegetables (+7.3%), meat and meat products (+5.9%), alcoholic beverages (+5.3%), musical instruments (+5.1%) and dairy products, eggs, edible oils and fats (+5.0%).

Conversely, price declines were seen from plastics and rubber in primary forms (-9.7%), waste and scrap (-9.5%), other liquid and gaseous fuels and related products (-8.7%), solid fuels (-8.6%), and motor spirit (-5.4%).

On a monthly basis, wholesale prices fell by 0.6% in August, reversing a 0.2% increase in the preceding period.

data.title: India's Concord Control hits record high on bonus issue plan, new rail propulsion system
data.description:
** Shares of Concord Control Systems jump 7.8% to a record high of 2,450 rupees

** The maker of electric and electronic products for Indian Railways will consider issue of bonus shares on September 7

** CONL also announces development of new zero-emission propulsion system for rail engines

** Nearly 26,000 shares traded, 4x their 30 day avg

** CONL stock up 46% so far in 2025

data.title: Austrian Economy Extends Growth in Q2
data.description:
The Austrian economy expanded by 0.3% quarter-on-quarter in Q2 2025, beating initial estimates of 0.1% and up from an upwardly revised 0.2% in the previous quarter.

This marked the second consecutive quarterly expansion after more than two years of stagnation or contraction, primarily driven by fixed investments (0.7% vs -0.3% in Q1), led by significant growth in machinery and transport equipment.

Net trade also contributed positively to the GDP, with exports rising 1% (vs 0.1%) and imports dropping 0.6% (vs 0.2%).

On the other hand, household consumption fell (-0.2% vs 0%), while government spending growth slowed sharply (0.1% vs 2.3%).

On the production side, growth was driven by gains in industry (manufacturing, including mining, energy and water supply), business-related services, and the public sector.

On a yearly basis, GDP fell by 0.1%, the softest in nine periods of decline, slowing from a 0.5% contraction in the preceding quarter.

data.title: Temenos shares fall after CEO ousting
data.description:
** Shares of Swiss banking software Temenos fall around 12% after the company announced on Thursday evening that it is parting ways with its CEO Jean-Pierre Brulard with immediate effect

** "Although these personnel decisions should not have any impact on the consensus estimates, they do significantly increase the uncertainty for the investment case," Zuercher Kantonalbank analysts say in a note

** They added the departure of Brulard after 16 months in office came as a "complete surprise"

** "Quite clearly there has been a difference in strategic views," Jefferies analysts say in a note, "Our interpretation is that the CEO wanted a bolder approach, while the Board wanted less disruption to teams and more stability"

** Shares of are down 11.8% to CHF 62.35 at 07:26 GMT, paring losses after falling as much as 14.1%, on track for worst day since February 2024

data.title: Gilt Yields Fall Ahead of U.S. Jobs Data — Market Talk
data.description: Yields on U.K. government bonds, or gilts, fall along with their eurozone peers ahead of key U.S. non-farm payrolls data due at 1230 GMT. Weak jobs data could increase expectations for U.S. interest-rate cuts in the coming months, starting in September. They come after U.S. job openings data and ADP private payrolls data came in weaker than expected. "A reading around consensus would be a sign that the U.S. labor market is slowing down sharply," XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. Gilts shrug off U.K. retail sales data showing a solid monthly rise during July but a weaker-than-expected annual increase. Ten-year gilt yields fall 3 basis points to last trade at 4.707%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Indian shares reverse course on profit-booking, dragged by IT, consumer stocks
data.description:
** Nifty 50 , Sensex slip ~0.4% each as investors book profits across sectors, reversing 0.3% early gains

** Barring autos , which extended rise on GST cuts, all 15 other major sectoral indexes fall

** Small- , mid-caps drop 0.3% each

** IT index slides 1.7%

** Speculation that U.S. may explore tariffs on Indian IT services spurred panic bids, dragging rupee to all-time low of 88.36, traders say

** Reuters could not verify if U.S. looking to explore IT tariffs

** Consumer stocks , up 2.7% over last five sessions, loses 1.6%

** ITC loses 2.3% after media report says government may impose fresh levy on tobacco products once 40% GST compensation cess expires

** Rapid GST progress sparked optimism, but we are "now seeing a classic 'sell-on-news' reaction, as markets have priced in the tax cuts," says Rajesh Bhosale, equity technical analyst at Angel One

** NSEI, BSESN up ~0.8% each for the week, led by NIFTYAUTO's 5% jump

data.title: Euro zone bond yields ease as focus turns to US payrolls report
data.description:
Euro zone government bond yields dipped on Friday, stabilising at the end to a volatile week, ahead of potentially disruptive U.S. data on monthly employment where a weak report could see Bunds lag Treasuries.

Germany's 10-year yield (DE10YT=RR), the benchmark for euro zone bonds, was down 2 basis points at 2.705%, cooling off from the five-month peak of 2.80% touched earlier in the week.

Similarly, the 30-year German yield (DE30YT=RR) was also down 2 bps at 3.324%, extending its decline after hitting a 14-year high of 3.434% on Wednesday, amidst a global selloff in long-duration government debt due to worries over the sustainability of public finances in developed economies.

Other regional bond yields, like those in France and Italy, were trading in line with their German counterparts.

Unstable politics and reduced demand from investors like pension funds for long-dated debt have also weighed on long-duration bonds this week, with markets now awaiting the outcome of a no-confidence vote in France on September 8.

In the near-term, weaker U.S. labour market indicators have helped cement expectations of a rate cut by the Federal Reserve this month, pulling long-dated yields below this week's multi-year highs.

Those expectations could be tested later in the day, if non-farm payrolls increase by more than expected. Economists polled by Reuters expect a rise of 75,000 jobs in August.

The rate-sensitive 2-year U.S. Treasury yield (US2YT=RR) was last a touch lower at 3.58%, lingering near its lowest level since May.

However, a payrolls report that comes in well below expectations, and reinforces the need for a Fed rate cut, could trigger a larger rally in Treasury prices. That could push yields down faster than those on German debt, which are factoring in almost no chance of another rate cut in the euro zone for the coming months.

data.title: UK's Ashmore bottoms FTSE midcaps after core profit miss
data.description:
** Shares of Ashmore fall as much as 14.7% to 145.9p

** Emerging markets-focused asset manager Ashmore is top loser on FTSE mid-cap index , which is up 0.3%

** Co's H2 2025 adjusted EBITDA comes in 22% below company-compiled consensus, while revenue misses by 7%, JPM analysts say

** The revenue miss reflects a 6% shortfall in management fees, driven by a lower-than-expected fee margin - JPM

** ASHM's annual adj pre-tax profit of 70.1 million pounds ($94.70 million) comes below the 105.4 million pounds from last year

** YTD, stock up 7% while FTSE mid-cap index gains 4.4%

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds swaps, call at 1:00 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.45% at 80,351, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index fell 0.4% to 24,634, as profit booking in consumer stocks and a drop in information technology offset a rally in autos after tax cuts.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee fell 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar at 88.3200, as traders remained jittery over news related to U.S. tariffs on India, while likely dollar-selling intervention by the Reserve Bank of India curbed sharper losses, traders said.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was at 98.8225 rupees, with the yield steady at 6.4945%, after rupee slipped to record low, offseting the sentiment boost on abating fiscal concerns.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 2 bps at 5.49%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was down 1 bp at 5.7350%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.60% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.50%.

data.title: Slovakia GDP Growth Softest in Over 2 Years
data.description:
Slovakia’s economy grew by 0.6% year-on-year in the second quarter of 2025, revising higher from initial estimates of 0.4%, but slowing from 0.9% in the previous quarter.

This marks the weakest pace since Q1 2023, as contractions in real estate (-4.5%), construction (-1.8%), and information and communication (-1.1%) weighed on overall performance.

On the expenditure side, domestic consumption supported growth, with household spending accelerating (2% vs 0.5% in Q1) and public sector consumption rising 2.2%.

Investment activity also recovered, as gross fixed capital formation increased 4.2% for the first time in a year, while net exports contributed positively, with exports up 3% and imports 2.9%, bringing the trade balance into surplus for the first time in a year.

On a seasonally adjusted quarterly basis, the economy grew 0.2%, following a 0.1% rise in Q1.

data.title: U.S. Futures, European Stocks Mixed
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.2% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average were flat. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 climbed 0.1% in morning trading. Hexagon Series B rose 6.2% and Bridgepoint Group added 4.8%. On the other hand, Temenos slipped 13.6%, and DKSH Holding dropped 3.8%. The FTSE 100 was flat. Other stocks in Europe were mixed as France's CAC 40 declined 0.2% and Germany's DAX climbed 0.1%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index fell 0.1% to 95.37.

In commodities, Brent crude fell 0.4% to $66.74 a barrel, and WTI crude dropped 0.4% to $63.21 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 0.1% to 32.45 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield fell by 1 basis point to 2.716% from 2.722%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury was flat at 4.166%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were up as Japan's Nikkei 225 index gained 1%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng climbed 1.4%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite gained 1.2%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.
